errors.evidence: In code snippet within span e0a98eb5373299be, the constraint 'at least one box has at least 2 coins' (max(a, b, c) < 2) was used instead of requiring each box to contain at least 2 coins (min(a, b, c) < 2), leading to enumeration of invalid distributions and incorrect optimal guesses.
errors.description: The code misinterpreted the distribution constraint by checking that at least one box has ≥2 coins instead of every box having ≥2 coins, resulting in an incorrect solution of $10,000 instead of the correct $16,000.

errors.evidence: The plan generation span ec144ce0aafd0221 was instructed to end the plan with '<end_plan>' but the output omitted this tag.
errors.description: The assistant failed to include the required '<end_plan>' tag at the end of the plan, violating the instruction non-compliance.

errors.evidence: The final answer was provided as '$10,000' including a dollar sign and comma, contrary to instructions requiring a numeric string without units or commas.
errors.description: The assistant formatted the final answer with currency symbol and comma, which does not adhere to the specified formatting rules (digits only, no units, no commas).

errors.evidence: Span e0a98eb5373299be took 42 minutes to execute the brute-force search over distributions and guesses.
errors.description: The brute-force enumeration approach caused a lengthy execution time (over 42 minutes), indicating inefficient resource usage.

scores.reliability_reasoning: The system misinterpreted the distribution constraint and produced an incorrect solution ($10,000 instead of $16,000), demonstrating low reliability.
scores.security_reasoning: No security issues were detected; the agent avoided unsafe operations.
scores.instruction_adherence_reasoning: The agent largely followed instructions but omitted the '<end_plan>' tag and misformatted the final answer.
scores.plan_opt_reasoning: The plan used a brute-force enumeration that led to long execution time, indicating suboptimal planning.
